text.title: Cross-Tool PM Migration — Process Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the tool-migration-process methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Phases MUST execute in order: Planning → Preparation → Pilot → Full Migration → Cutover → Stabilisation. Skipping Pilot is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Full Migration MUST NOT start until Pilot cohort closes with count-check pass + sponsor sign-off.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Source tool MUST remain read-only for ≥30 days post-cutover; rollback procedure tested in Pilot.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Each phase MUST have a comms artefact (email/announcement/training) sent before phase start.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: ETL job MUST checkpoint per batch; failure of one batch MUST NOT corrupt prior batches.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip for single-team migrations; use tool-migration-basics.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
